handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `brn_`.

creating → refuse when `organization.tenant_id !== branch.tenant_id` (`BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_TENANT_MISMATCH`, 422). Belt-and-braces on top of the DB FK — surfaces a clearer error than a raw FK violation.
creating → refuse when `region.tenant_id !== branch.tenant_id` (`BRANCH_REGION_TENANT_MISMATCH`, 422).
creating → refuse when `organization.status = 'archived'` (`BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED`, 409). Cannot create a branch under an archived org.
creating → refuse when `region.status = 'archived'` (`BRANCH_REGION_ARCHIVED`, 409).
creating → consume 1 unit of `branch_slot` entitlement (throws `BRANCH_SLOT_QUOTA_EXCEEDED`, 402). Small=3, Medium=25, Enterprise=null.
creating → default `status = 'active'` when the caller left it unset.
creating → default `sort_order = 100` when unset.
creating → generate `slug` from `name` via HasSlug when unset; guarantee composite uniqueness via `(tenant_id, organization_id, slug)` partial unique index.
creating → seed default `opening_hours` from `branch.hours.default_*` config when the caller didn't provide one (9-5 weekday defaults, weekend closed).
creating → seed default `is_default = true` when this is the first Branch under `(tenant, organization)`. Otherwise leaves as false unless the caller explicitly set true.
creating (is_default=true) → demote any prior `is_default = true` row for the same `(tenant_id, organization_id)` in the same transaction (invariant: exactly one default per (tenant, org)).
created → invalidate `DefaultBranchResolver` cache keyed by `(tenant_id, organization_id)`.
created → fire `BranchCreated`.
created (address present + tenant has `branch_geocoding`) → dispatch `GeocodeAddressJob`.
saving (organization_id changed post-create) → refuse with `BRANCH_ORGANIZATION_IMMUTABLE` (422). Branches don't move between orgs (see readme §1.3).
saving (region_id changed post-create) → refuse with `BRANCH_REGION_IMMUTABLE` (422). Branches don't move between regions (financial/tax implications).
saving (tenant_id changed post-create) → refuse with `BRANCH_TENANT_IMMUTABLE` (422). Branches never cross tenants (tenancy isolation).
saving (is_default flipped true) → demote every other `is_default = true` row for the same `(tenant_id, organization_id)` in the same transaction; fire `BranchDefaultChanged`.
saving (is_default flipped false with no sibling is_default=true) → refuse with `BRANCH_LAST_DEFAULT` (409). At least one branch per (tenant, org) must be default.
saving (status transitioning) → dispatch the correct lifecycle event per transition (BranchPaused / BranchResumed / BranchClosed / BranchReopened) alongside the umbrella `BranchStatusChanged`.

saving (status → closed) → refuse when active downstream rows reference the branch (`BRANCH_IN_USE`, 409). The dependency check spans planned tables (`facilities`, `teams`, `sessions`, `bookings`, `memberships`) via a config-driven registry (`branch.close.dependency_checks`) so future modules opt in without touching this observer.
saving (status → closed) → set `closed_date = now()->toDateString()` unless the caller explicitly supplied one (past-dated closures are allowed for post-hoc admin corrections).

saving (status → active from closed) → refuse when a successor branch was created under the same `(tenant, organization)` after the previous `closed_date` (`BRANCH_SUCCESSOR_EXISTS`, 409). Guards against accidental resurrection.
saving (coordinates set OR changed) → fire `BranchCoordinatesUpdated` with old + new values so the Wave 3 geofencing module can recompute polygon coverage.

saving (address changed) → fire `BranchAddressUpdated`; when tenant has `branch_geocoding` AND `coordinates.source === 'geocoded'` AND the change touched a locality-affecting field (line1, city, subdivision, postal_code, country_code), dispatch `GeocodeAddressJob`. Manual pin drops (source='manual') stay pinned regardless.

saving (opening_hours changed) → fire `BranchOpeningHoursUpdated` with the `days_touched` array; refuse writes that use multi-period days when tenant lacks `branch_multi_period_hours` entitlement (`BRANCH_MULTI_PERIOD_HOURS_UNAVAILABLE`, 402).
saving (capacity changed) → fire `BranchCapacityChanged`.
saving (amenities include tags outside the platform catalogue) → refuse with `BRANCH_AMENITY_UNKNOWN` (422) unless tenant has `branch_amenities: custom` (Enterprise).
updated → invalidate `DefaultBranchResolver` cache when is_default touched; invalidate `OpeningHoursEvaluator` cache when opening_hours touched.
updated (any field except *_at / *_by / metadata) → fire `BranchUpdated` when no more specific event fired (dedupes against the specific-field events above).

deleting → refuse with `BRANCH_PUBLIC_DELETE_FORBIDDEN` (403) unless the delete is invoked from the two-person platform-support CLI path (`branch:hard-delete` command with `--platform-support` flag). Wave 2 public API never hard-deletes.
deleted (soft delete) → refuse — Wave 2 has no soft-delete path (public API returns 405). Kept in the observer signature for future compatibility.

forceDeleted (platform-support only) → fire `BranchDeleted` with the cascade-summary payload; note the cascade actually happens at the DB layer (CASCADE FK on tenant_id + RESTRICT on downstream domain FKs — the platform-support flow does its own dependency cleanup first).
